title: Hippeastrum andalgalense
taxonomy: Hippeastrum andalgalense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. It is a type of bulbous perennial that is native to the Andes mountain range in South America. The plant belongs to the genus Hippeastrum, which comprises about 90 species of flowering plants. Hippeastrum andalgalense is further classified within the subgenus Hippeastrum section Lenophora. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Amaryllidaceae family.

side_effects: Hippeastrum andalgalense, also known as the Andalgalan amaryllis, is a plant that is generally non-toxic to humans and pets. However, its bulbs contain toxic compounds that can cause gastrointestinal symptoms if ingested. intro: Hippeastrum andalgalense, commonly known as Andalgalense Amaryllis, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Hippeastrum andalgalense include alkaloids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
